page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz with no hyperthreading, ensuring dedicated compute resources per vCPU. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For high-speed local storage, it includes 11,400 GB of bundled NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, cached and uncached memory bandwidth, and PassMark database operations. It also demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation capabilities. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average tier. Additionally, LLM inference prompt processing for small models is relatively weak, though prompt processing for medium models registers as strong.

Given its high resource density and ARM64 architecture, the m8gd.48xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage its 192 physical cores and massive 11.4 TB local NVMe storage. The absence of hyperthreading reduces resource contention, making this profile highly suited for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput web serving, Redis caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing or software compilation pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose instance powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.7 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of memory and a substantial 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the Nitro hypervisor with 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside strong software compilation and multi-threaded decompression capabilities. However, single-core performance and small-model LLM inference prompt processing are relatively average or weak. This hardware configuration is highly efficient for parallelized workloads, making it well-suited for large databases, high-throughput static web serving, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded build pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 192 Graviton cores, 768 GB RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

Equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM and 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
